item.title: WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: New AI agents on WordPress.com could lower barriers to publishing while increasing machine-generated content across the web.

item.title: Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

item.title: The best AI investment might be in energy tech
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Power has become one of the biggest bottlenecks in rolling out new AI data centers. That's creating an opening for investors.

item.title: These AI notetaking devices can help you record and transcribe your meetings
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: These physical notetakers transcribe audio and give users summaries and action items of meetings using AI. Some even offer live translation.

item.title: Jeff Bezos reportedly wants $100 billion to buy and transform old manufacturing firms with AI
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The Amazon magnate has a new project centered around acquiring industrial firms and revamping them with AI technology.

item.title: Online bot traffic will exceed human traffic by 2027, Cloudflare CEO says
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: AI bots may outnumber humans online by 2027, says Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince, as generative AI agents dramatically increase web traffic and infrastructure demands.

item.title: Meta rolls out new AI content enforcement systems while reducing reliance on third-party vendors
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Meta believes these AI systems can detect more violations with greater accuracy, better prevent scams, respond more quickly to real-world events, and reduce over-enforcement.

item.title: DoorDash launches a new ‘Tasks’ app that pays couriers to submit videos to train AI
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Delivery couriers will be able to earn money by completing activities like filming everyday tasks or recording themselves speaking in another language.

item.title: TechCrunch Startup Battlefield 200 nominations are still open
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Nominate your startup, or one you know, for TechCrunch Startup Battlefield 200 before May 27. Chance to win $100,000 equity-free funding and VC access.

item.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the UK
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The company is currently letting users in the U.K. try out Alexa+ for free via an early access program.

item.title: Multiverse Computing pushes its compressed AI models into the mainstream
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: After compressing models from major AI labs, including OpenAI, Meta, DeepSeek, and Mistral AI, Multiverse Computing has launched both an app that showcases the capabilities of its compressed models and an API that makes them more widely available.

item.title: Meta is having trouble with rogue AI agents
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: A rogue AI agent inadvertently exposed Meta company and user data to engineers who didn't have permission to see it.

item.title: Sam Altman’s thank-you to coders draws the memes
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Altman expresses gratitude for people who knew how to write their code from scratch. The internet replies with salty jokes.

item.title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says smartphone apps will disappear as AI agents take their place
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says AI agents will eventually replace apps, shifting smartphones toward systems that understand intent and act on a user's behalf.

item.title: Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Nvidia's networking business raked in $11 billion last quarter despite getting significantly less fanfare than chips and gaming.

item.title: Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Patreon CEO Jack Conte says AI companies should pay creators for training data, arguing their fair use defense falls apart when they license content from major publishers.

item.title: Rebel Audio is a new AI podcasting tool aimed at first-time creators
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Rebel Audio is a new all-in-one podcasting tool that allows creators to record podcasts, edit, clip content for social, and publish episodes, all without ever leaving the platform.

item.title: The Gemini-powered features in Google Workspace that are worth using
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: From summarizing emails, drafting content, organizing data, and tracking meetings, here are all the best Gemini features in Google Workspace.

item.title: The leaderboard “you can’t game,” funded by the companies it ranks
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Artificial intelligence models are multiplying fast, and competition is stiff. With so many players crowding the space, which one will be the best — and who decides that? Arena, formerly LM Arena, has emerged as the de facto public leaderboard for frontier LLMs, influencing funding, launches, and PR cycles. In just seven months, the startup went from a UC Berkeley PhD research […]

item.title: This startup wants to make enterprise software look more like a prompt
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company has raised $12 million in seed funding to build an AI operating system for enterprise.
